workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Can you work with me on executive brief for Fourth Coffee?

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-06 08:37 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

Our team encountered some challenges with executive brief. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-01-25 13:56 EST
Subject: Follow-up: executive brief Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about executive brief. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Fourth Coffee: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-O trap (MSX delta requires approval for obvious updates (next-step changes)); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-O failure mode (MSX delta requires approval for obvious updates (next-step changes))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-O -- MSX delta requires approval for obvious updates (next-step changes)
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
